title: polygala vulgaris subsp. involutiflora
intro: Polygala vulgaris subsp. involutiflora is a subspecies of the Polygala vulgaris plant, commonly known as the common polygala or running milkwort, a perennial herb native to Europe and Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, as it is traditionally used to treat respiratory issues and has been reported to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Polygala vulgaris subsp. involutiflora include its potential to alleviate symptoms of bronchitis, asthma, and other respiratory conditions, as well as its ability to boost the immune system and protect against oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tincture or tea, and it is also used in combination with other herbs to create remedies for respiratory and cardiovascular health issues.
